Petition To Determine Heirship
Off Calendar Aug-20-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Owens for Petitioner. Jonathan Ferdon for San Francisco Public Administrator.

The Court expressed concern about Mr. Istvan Nagy's understanding that a copy of a will can be probated. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Y. Watts.

Petition To Determine Heirship
Off Calendar Jul-14-2010 Continued To Jul-20-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Owens for Eva Wallner.

Mr. Owens stated that he spoke with Jonathan Ferdon and that Mr. Ferdon stated that he may or may not appear at today's hearing. Mr. Owens stepped out of the courtroom, placed a call to Mr. Ferdon's office and returned stating that the call went to voice mail. The matter was continued to July 20, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 as the Court would like to hear from Mr. Ferdon with regard to any responses, the inability to deliver letters or any e mails coming back. A declaration from Mr. Ferdon with regard to the above would be acceptable.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.

Petition To Determine Heirship
Off Calendar Jul-07-2010 Continued To Jul-14-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Heirship
Off Calendar May-05-2010 Continued To Jul-07-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the Administrator of the Estate. David Owens for Eva Wallner (Petitioner).

Mr. Ferdon provided a copy of the will he received from Mr. Kaltroy who resides in Hungary for the Court's review. Mr. Ferdon stated that it was only a copy, that he wrote back to Mr. Kaltroy and to the Witnesses asking if they knew where the original will was but that he never heard back from anyone, and that there was no way for his Client to be considered custodians of the will and therefore did not lodge it. Mr. Owens stated that the Decedent's birth records did not list a person as the Father, that the Social Security application completed 32 years later listed Mr. Nagy as the Father, that his position was that the Decedent was born out of wedlock, stated that there was no way to trace Paternal Heirs and that he did not know if a search for a marriage license was done. The Court stated that it was not sure if Mr. Kaltroy had a clear understanding of what was going to happen. The Court ordered Mr. Ferdon to send a letter to Mr. Kaltroy once again at the last know address he provided giving him a clear indication that if he wishes to pursue the matter that he needs to contact an Attorney. If the Court does not hear from him by July 7, 2010, the Court will proceed to act on the Petition to Determine Heirship. Mr. Ferdon was also ordered to send the contents of the letter by way of e-mail to the e-mail address provided by Mr. Kaltroy. Mr. Ferdon was further ordered to file copies of the letters sent to the Witnesses and copies of the letters and the e-mail sent to Mr. Kaltroy. The matter was continued to July 7, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
